wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the 99-Pai project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as a unified elderly care management and service marketplace solution. Document the technology stack including NestJS, TypeScript, Prisma ORM, and PostgreSQL. Describe the core value proposition of connecting elderly users with caregivers and service providers through a centralized API. Include architectural overview showing the modular design pattern, authentication system, and data flow. Provide context about the target audience (elderly care facilities, independent elderly users, caregivers, service providers) and their workflows. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the 99-Pai API. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js, PostgreSQL, Prisma CLI), step-by-step installation instructions, environment configuration, database setup and seeding. Document the development workflow including npm scripts, running in watch mode, and accessing Swagger documentation. Provide initial API testing examples with curl commands or Postman collection. Include troubleshooting for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make the content accessible to developers new to NestJS while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the 99-Pai platform. Detail the JWT-based authentication flow including token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Document the multi-role system (elderly, caregiver, provider, admin) with role-based access control implementation. Explain the Passport.js integration, custom JWT strategy, and guard patterns. Include DTO validation for login and signup operations, password hashing with bcrypt, and security best practices. Provide practical examples of protected routes, role decorators, and permission checking. Document the relationship between authentication and authorization with concrete code examples from the actual implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema & Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the 99-Pai database schema. Detail all entity relationships defined in Prisma schema including Users, ElderlyProfiles, Caregivers, Offerings, ServiceRequests, and supporting entities. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain the hierarchical category system, medication tracking relationships, and service marketplace data flows. Include Prisma service implementation, automatic connection management, and database migration strategies. Document seed data structure and initialization process. Provide entity relationship diagrams and sample data examples. Address data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Elderly Care Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the elderly care management system. Cover the four core subsystems: elderly profile management, medication tracking, contact management, and agenda scheduling. Document the implementation patterns including CRUD operations, validation rules, and business logic. Explain the relationships between these systems and how they integrate to provide comprehensive care coordination. Include detailed API documentation for each module with request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Provide practical examples of common workflows such as medication scheduling, emergency contact updates, and appointment management. Address data privacy considerations and caregiver access permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Caregiver Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the caregiver integration system. Explain the caregiver registration and linking process, including verification mechanisms and access control. Document the caregiver-elderly relationship management, permission models, and data access patterns. Detail the implementation of caregiver-specific features such as care statistics, access verification, and care activity tracking. Include API documentation for caregiver operations, linking workflows, and permission management. Provide examples of common caregiver scenarios including profile management, access requests, and care coordination. Address security considerations and audit trails for caregiver activities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Marketplace
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the service marketplace system. Detail the three core components: category management with hierarchical relationships, service offerings with provider management, and service requests with status tracking. Document the marketplace architecture including category navigation, offering creation and management, and request processing workflows. Explain the provider-client relationship dynamics, pricing systems, and communication patterns. Include detailed API documentation for marketplace operations, search and filtering capabilities, and transaction management. Provide examples of common marketplace scenarios including service discovery, request submission, and provider assignment. Address administrative features and marketplace analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Communication Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the communication systems within the 99-Pai platform. Cover the three main communication channels: push notifications with token management and platform support, interaction logging for care activity tracking, and weather integration for location-based services. Document the implementation of notification delivery mechanisms, interaction type classification, and weather data integration. Include API documentation for communication endpoints, notification templates, and interaction recording. Provide examples of common communication scenarios including care reminders, service updates, and weather alerts. Address platform-specific considerations for iOS/Android/web notification delivery and data privacy for communication logs.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for all 99-Pai endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each controller. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and example requests/responses. Organize endpoints by functional area (authentication, elderly care, marketplace, communication) with clear navigation. Document error codes, status responses, and common failure scenarios. Provide curl examples and Postman collection links for easy testing. Include rate limiting information, pagination patterns, and filtering capabilities. Address CORS configuration, content-type requirements, and API versioning strategy. Make the documentation searchable and cross-referenced for easy navigation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing and deployment documentation for the 99-Pai platform. Document the testing strategy including unit tests with Jest, integration tests, and end-to-end testing approaches. Explain test configuration, mocking strategies, and test coverage requirements. Include deployment documentation covering environment configuration, database migrations, Docker deployment options, and production readiness checks. Document CI/CD pipeline considerations, monitoring setup, and logging strategies. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and performance optimization techniques. Address security considerations for production environments and backup/recovery procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for the 99-Pai project. Document code style standards, ESLint configuration, and Prettier formatting rules. Explain the project structure conventions, naming patterns, and architectural guidelines. Include contribution guidelines, pull request processes, and code review standards. Document TypeScript configuration, build processes, and development workflow. Provide guidelines for adding new features, extending existing modules, and maintaining code quality. Address version control practices, branching strategies, and release management. Include troubleshooting guides for common development issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# 99-Pai: Unified Elderly Assistant and Service Marketplace API

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
99-Pai is a comprehensive backend API service designed to provide unified care coordination and service marketplace functionality for elderly individuals and their caregivers. The platform integrates two distinct systems - "99por1" (elderly care management) and "PAI" (service marketplace) - into a single cohesive solution.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Care Coordination**: Manage elderly care schedules, medication tracking, and emergency contacts
- **Service Marketplace**: Enable elderly users to discover and request services from providers
- **Caregiver Integration**: Facilitate secure connections between elderly individuals and their designated caregivers
- **Real-time Communication**: Support push notifications and interaction logging for enhanced care delivery

### Target Audience
- Elderly individuals seeking care coordination and service access
- Caregivers managing multiple elderly profiles
- Healthcare providers offering specialized services
- Service providers in the elderly care ecosystem

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular NestJS architecture with clear separation of concerns across different functional domains:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Application] --> B[Authentication Module]
    A --> C[Elderly Management Module]
    A --> D[Caregiver Module]
    A --> E[Medications Module]
    A --> F[Contacts Module]
    A --> G[Agenda Module]
    A --> H[Notifications Module]
    A --> I[Interactions Module]
    A --> J[Weather Module]
    A --> K[Categories Module]
    A --> L[Offerings Module]
    A --> M[Service Requests Module]
    
    N[Data Layer] --> O[Prisma ORM]
    O --> P[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    B --> N
    C --> N
    D --> N
    E --> N
    F --> N
    G --> N
    H --> N
    I --> N
    J --> N
    K --> N
    L --> N
    M --> N
```

### Component Breakdown
The architecture consists of 14 distinct feature modules, each handling specific aspects of the elderly care ecosystem:

- **Core Modules**: Authentication, Prisma data layer, and Swagger documentation
- **Care Management**: Elderly profiles, medications, contacts, agenda scheduling
- **Service Ecosystem**: Categories, offerings, service requests, provider marketplace
- **Support Systems**: Notifications, interactions logging, weather integration

### Design Patterns
- **Module Pattern**: Each feature area encapsulated in dedicated modules
- **Repository Pattern**: Prisma service abstracts database operations
- **DTO Pattern**: Strongly typed data transfer objects for API contracts
- **Guard Pattern**: Role-based access control for sensitive operations

### System Relationships
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant AuthModule
    participant ElderlyModule
    participant MedicationsModule
    participant CaregiverModule
    
    Client->>+AuthModule: Login/Signup
    AuthModule->>+AuthModule: JWT Token Generation
    AuthModule-->>-Client: Authenticated Response
    
    Client->>+ElderlyModule: Get Profile
    ElderlyModule->>+CaregiverModule: Verify Caregiver Access
    CaregiverModule-->>-ElderlyModule: Access Verification
    ElderlyModule-->>-Client: Profile Data
    
    Client->>+MedicationsModule: Manage Medications
    MedicationsModule->>+MedicationsModule: Validate Permissions
    MedicationsModule-->>-Client: Medication Status
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
The application bootstraps through a centralized entry point that configures global middleware, security, and API documentation:

**Main Application Bootstrap**
- File: `src/main.ts`
- Configures global prefix, CORS, validation pipes, and Swagger documentation
- Sets up production-ready error handling and logging

**Application Module Configuration**
- File: `src/app.module.ts`
- Imports all feature modules and configures global dependencies
- Establishes the modular architecture foundation

### Core Modules
**Authentication System**
- File: `src/auth/auth.module.ts`
- Implements JWT-based authentication with passport strategies
- Manages user registration, login, and role-based access control

**Data Access Layer**
- File: `src/prisma/prisma.module.ts`
- Provides global Prisma service for database operations
- Abstracts database connection and query execution

**Medication Management**
- File: `src/medications/medications.module.ts`
- Handles medication scheduling, tracking, and confirmation workflows
- Integrates with caregiver verification system

### Configuration Approach
**Database Schema Definition**
- File: `prisma/schema.prisma`
- Defines comprehensive entity relationships including users, elderly profiles, medications, and service offerings
- Supports complex queries across multiple domains

**Package Dependencies**
- File: `package.json`
- NestJS 11.x with TypeScript support
- Prisma 6.x for modern database operations
- JWT authentication and validation libraries

### External Dependencies
Key external integrations include:
- **Prisma Client**: Modern database ORM with type safety
- **JWT Authentication**: Secure token-based user authentication
- **Passport.js**: Flexible authentication middleware
- **Class Validator**: Runtime validation and sanitization
- **Swagger**: API documentation generation

### Integration Points
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[External Services] --> B[Weather API]
    A --> C[Push Notification Service]
    A --> D[Payment Gateway]
    
    B --> E[Weather Module]
    C --> F[Notifications Module]
    D --> G[Service Requests Module]
    
    E --> H[Service Integration]
    F --> H
    G --> H
```

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
classDiagram
    class User {
        +String id
        +String email
        +Role role
        +elderlyProfile
        +caregiverLinks
        +pushTokens
    }
    
    class ElderlyProfile {
        +String userId
        +String linkCode
        +Boolean onboardingComplete
        +medications
        +contacts
        +agendaEvents
    }
    
    class Medication {
        +String elderlyProfileId
        +String name
        +String time
        +String dosage
        +Boolean active
    }
    
    class CaregiverLink {
        +String caregiverUserId
        +String elderlyProfileId
    }
    
    User ||--o{ ElderlyProfile : manages
    ElderlyProfile ||--o{ Medication : contains
    User ||--o{ CaregiverLink : connects
    ElderlyProfile ||--o{ CaregiverLink : links
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The platform provides comprehensive elderly care management with integrated service marketplace capabilities:

**Care Coordination Features**
- Medication tracking with daily confirmation system
- Contact management with automated call reminders
- Personalized agenda scheduling with recurring events
- Caregiver assignment and verification system
- Interaction logging for care quality metrics

**Service Marketplace Features**
- Hierarchical category system for service organization
- Provider offering management with pricing
- Service request processing workflow
- Contact management between requesters and providers
- Real-time availability and scheduling

### Implementation Highlights

**Multi-Domain Data Model**
- File: `prisma/schema.prisma`
- Supports both elderly care (99por1) and service marketplace (PAI) domains
- Complex relationships enabling seamless integration between domains

**Role-Based Access Control**
- File: `src/auth/auth.service.ts`
- Implements fine-grained permissions for different user types
- Ensures caregivers can only access assigned elderly profiles

**Medication Management Workflow**
- File: `src/medications/medications.service.ts`
- Comprehensive medication lifecycle management
- Automated history tracking and status updates
- Caregiver notification system for missed doses

### Feature Architecture Diagram
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> UserRegistration
    UserRegistration --> UserLogin
    UserLogin --> ProfileManagement
    ProfileManagement --> CareCoordination
    ProfileManagement --> ServiceMarketplace
    
    CareCoordination --> MedicationTracking
    CareCoordination --> ContactManagement
    CareCoordination --> AgendaScheduling
    
    ServiceMarketplace --> CategoryBrowsing
    ServiceMarketplace --> ServiceRequests
    ServiceMarketplace --> ProviderManagement
    
    MedicationTracking --> CaregiverNotification
    CaregiverNotification --> [*]
    
    CategoryBrowsing --> ServiceRequests
    ServiceRequests --> ProviderResponse
    ProviderResponse --> [*]
```

**Integration Architecture**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Applications] --> B[REST API Layer]
    B --> C[Business Logic Layer]
    C --> D[Data Access Layer]
    D --> E[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    F[External Services] --> G[Weather API]
    F --> H[Push Notification Service]
    F --> I[Payment Processing]
    
    G --> C
    H --> C
    I --> C
    
    C --> J[Response Formatting]
    J --> B
    B --> A
```

---

**Sources:**
- [main.ts](src/main.ts)
- [app.module.ts](src/app.module.ts)
- [schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [auth.module.ts](src/auth/auth.module.ts)
- [elderly.module.ts](src/elderly/elderly.module.ts)
- [medications.module.ts](src/medications/medications.module.ts)
- [offerings.module.ts](src/offerings/offerings.module.ts)
- [prisma.module.ts](src/prisma/prisma.module.ts)
- [login.dto.ts](src/auth/dto/login.dto.ts)
- [create-medication.dto.ts](src/medications/dto/create-medication.dto.ts)
- [create-offering.dto.ts](src/offerings/dto/create-offering.dto.ts)
- [auth.service.ts](src/auth/auth.service.ts)
- [medications.service.ts](src/medications/medications.service.ts)
- [package.json](package.json)

</blog>
